payload.message:
Run this command: sh ~/.openclaw/workspace-security/scripts/audit-lulu.sh

Read the output. Then reply with your security report as a normal message.

DO NOT use the write tool. DO NOT use the edit tool. DO NOT call any tools to write files. Just reply directly with plain text.
NEVER say HEARTBEAT_OK anywhere in your reply. ALWAYS send the full report below.

KNOWN SAFE TRAFFIC (ignore these):
- Google/Chrome on ports 443, 5228
- Discord on port 443 to 162.159.x.x
- Telegram on port 443 to 149.154.x.x
- node (OpenClaw) on port 443
- Apple services (17.x.x.x) on ports 443, 5223
- Ollama, LuLu, mDNSResponder, trustd, ocspd, apsd, cloudd
- localhost connections (127.0.0.1)

FLAG AS SUSPICIOUS:
- Unknown processes making outbound connections
- Connections to unusual ports
- Connections to Tor exit nodes or known-bad IPs

ALWAYS reply in this exact format:

Security Audit Report — 2026-03-09

New outbound IPs detected:
- (list any non-standard IPs with process and port, or 'none')

Suspicious activity:
- (describe anything unusual, or 'none')

Risk level: None/Low/Medium/High/Critical

Recommended actions:
- (what to do, or 'none needed')
I want a brief of the latest tech news. Important: only the news from the last 24 hours.

Refresh the cuiz-ai HTML dashboard in /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace-analytics by running ./scripts/update_cuiz_dashboard.py from the workspace root. Do not change the script unless it is broken. Update the dashboard files in /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace-analytics/dashboard. If the refresh succeeds, finish quietly. If it fails, report the failure with the concrete error.
